text:
Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim was very happy to go to school. He loved to learn new things every day. One sunny morning, Tim said to his friend, "I want to learn about animals today!"

His friend, Sam, smiled and said, "I want to learn, too! Let’s ask our teacher!" So, they rushed to their teacher, Miss Lee. Tim said, "Miss Lee, can we learn about animals?" Miss Lee clapped her hands and said, "Yes! Let’s all learn about animals!"

In the classroom, Miss Lee showed them pictures of big elephants and tiny mice. "Look at this one! What is it?" she asked. The kids shouted, "Elephant!" Everyone giggled and had fun. Tim felt so happy as he learned about animals with his friends.

At the end of the day, Tim said, "I learned so much!" Sam nodded and said, "Me too! I love school!" They both smiled and ran home, thinking about all the fun they had. Tim was very happy to learn at school that day.

One bright day in the classroom, Anna had her crayons. She loved to draw. Anna liked to draw flowers, trees, and happy faces. Her picture was big and colorful. 

But then, Anna saw her friend Tom. He looked sad. Tom did not have crayons. He wanted to draw too. Anna thought for a moment. She wanted to help him. So, she smiled and said, "You can use my crayons!"

Tom's face lit up. He took a red crayon, and they both started to draw together. Anna drew a big sun, and Tom drew a blue car. They laughed and helped each other. The classroom was filled with bright colors and happy sounds.

When they finished, their pictures were together on the wall. The teacher smiled and said, "Great job, Anna and Tom!" Anna felt happy inside. She learned that sharing made fun times even better. They played and drew together every day after that.

One sunny day at school, Sam was very happy. It was time to go to the playground! Sam ran to his friend, Lily. “Let’s go and play!” he said with a big smile.

At the playground, they saw a big slide and swings. “I want to go on the slide!” said Lily. “Okay! I will read a book first!” Sam said. He took out his book and sat on a bench. It was a kind book about a dog and a cat. 

Lily climbed the slide and shouted, “Look at me, Sam!” Sam looked up and smiled. “You are so high! I will come and play after I read!” 

When Sam finished his book, he ran to the slide. “Now I can play too!” he said. They laughed and had fun all day. It was a happy time at school!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy went to a big school with many kids. She liked her school very much. Her teacher was nice, and she always said to be kind and help others. 

One day, Lucy saw her friend Tim. Tim looked sad because he could not write his name. Lucy wanted to help him. She said, "Don't worry, Tim! I can help you." Tim smiled a little. 

Lucy found a nice, quiet spot in the room. She took a paper and a big pencil. "Let’s write together," she said. Lucy wrote the big letters, and Tim traced them with his finger. Tim felt happy and started to try on his own. 

Soon, Tim wrote his name all by himself! He jumped up and down with joy. "Look, Lucy! I did it!" Lucy clapped her hands and cheered. They both smiled, and the teacher saw them. She was proud of Lucy for helping Tim. They learned that helping each other is a great way to be happy!

Jake was a shy boy. He liked to draw with his pencil. One day at school, he sat at his desk and drew a big red sun. He felt happy when he drew. 

Then, his friend Mia came to him. "Jake, can you help me?" she asked. She was sad because she could not find her blue pencil. Jake looked around. 

"I will help you!" Jake said. He stood up and began to run. He looked under the desk and behind the door. At last, he found the blue pencil! 

"Look, Mia! I found it!" Jake shouted with a smile. Mia's face lit up. "Thank you, Jake! You are the best!" They both sat down and drew together. Jake felt happy because he helped his friend. They had fun and played all day.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Mia. Mia was very curious. She loved to learn new things. One day at school, Mia saw a big book on the shelf. It had bright pictures and fun words. Mia wanted to play with the book.

Mia picked up the book and opened it. The pictures were of happy animals and sunny days. Mia showed the book to her friends. They all sat together and looked at the book. They laughed and played as they shared the stories inside. Mia felt happy to share her find.

Then, the teacher came over. She smiled and said, “This is great! It is fun to share a book with friends.” Mia felt proud. She learned that sharing makes everyone happy. 

At the end of the day, Mia and her friends read many stories together. They played with the book and had a wonderful time. Mia went home with a big smile, ready to tell her family about her fun day at school. She knew that sharing is a nice way to play!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ben. Ben was a brave boy who loved to help his friends. One day at school, the teacher said that everyone should share their toys at the big desk in the room. Ben was excited! 

He took out his favorite toy car and placed it on the desk. Then he looked around. He saw that his friend Mia was sad because she didn’t have any toys to share. Ben smiled and said, “You can play with my car, Mia!” Mia's face lit up with joy. 

Ben and Mia played together, making the car go zoom-zoom! Soon, other kids joined them at the desk. They all shared their toys. There were dolls, blocks, and train sets. Everyone was happy and laughing. 

At the end of the day, the teacher said, “You all did a great job sharing!” Ben felt proud. He was brave for helping Mia and making sure everyone had fun. They all went home with big smiles and warm hearts.

Zoe went to school one day. She was very happy. At school, the kids played and learned. It was time for lunch. Zoe had a small lunch box. 

Zoe opened her lunch box. “Yum!” she said. She had a sandwich and an apple. But her friend, Sam, looked sad. “What is wrong?” Zoe asked. Sam said, “I forgot my lunch.” 

Zoe smiled. "I can help!" She took out her sandwich and gave half to Sam. Then she gave him her apple, too. Sam was happy. “Thank you, Zoe!” he said with a big smile. 

The two friends ate together and shared stories. They talked and laughed. At the end of lunch, they felt good. Zoe was happy to help her friend. The day ended with joy, and they promised to play together later!

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Leo. Leo had a gentle smile and a big backpack full of fun things. Every day, he brought his backpack to school, ready to learn and play with his friends.

One sunny day, Leo saw his friend Mia sitting alone. She looked sad because she lost her favorite toy. Leo wanted to help her. He took a deep breath and began to sing a happy song. The song was soft and gentle, like a warm hug. Mia listened and started to smile.

“Let’s look for your toy!” Leo said. Together, they walked around the school, looking here and there. Leo kept singing his gentle song. Soon, they found the toy behind a tree! Mia jumped with joy and hugged Leo tightly. 

“Thank you, Leo!” Mia said, her eyes shining. Leo felt warm inside. Helping his friend made him happy. They walked back to class, singing together, and they knew that friends are the best gifts of all. And they had a wonderful day at school!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Ella. Ella was clever and loved to learn. One day, her teacher said, “Let’s build a big tower with blocks!” Ella was very excited. She took her red crayon and drew a picture of the tower she wanted to build. 

Ella had a fun idea. She thought, “I can help my friends!” So, she asked her friends to join her. They all took blocks and started to build together. They laughed and shared, making the tower bigger and bigger. Ella used her crayon to mark where to put each block. The tower was tall and strong!

Then, Ella looked at the tower and saw something special. It was not just a tower; it was a team tower! Ella and her friends worked together, and that made their tower great. They all felt happy and proud. 

The teacher came to see their work and smiled. She said, "You all are clever and worked as a team!" Ella learned that building together is fun. That day, they made a big, happy tower and a lot of new friends.

Once upon a time, in a big school, there was a boy named Max. Max was very happy. He loved to go to the library. The library had many books and was a very cheerful place. Today, Max had a special idea. He wanted to paint a big picture!

Max said to his friend, "Can you help me paint?" His friend smiled and said, "Yes! Let’s make it bright and fun!" They took out their paints and brushes. Max chose red, yellow, and blue. They worked hard. They laughed as they made swirls and dots on the big paper.

When they finished, their picture was very pretty. It showed the sun, flowers, and happy kids. Max and his friend looked at it with big smiles. "Let’s show everyone!" said Max. They ran to the library and showed their work to the teacher. The teacher clapped her hands and said, "What a cheerful picture! You made the library even better!"

The moral of the story is: When you share and work together, you can make something great! Max felt proud and happy. Everyone in the library smiled because of the beautiful picture. Max and his friend had fun, and they made the school a brighter place.

Lily was a kind girl at school. She had a big smile and liked to help her friends. One day, Lily saw her friend Ben looking sad. He had a lot of homework to do, and he did not know how to start.

Lily said, "Don't worry, Ben! I can help you." Ben looked up and smiled. "Thank you, Lily! You are so friendly!" They sat together at a big table. Lily took out her notebook, and they began to count the things Ben had to do. 

They counted numbers, letters, and words. Lily showed Ben how to write. Ben listened carefully and tried his best. Soon, they finished the homework together! 

Ben felt happy and said, "I can do this now!" Lily clapped her hands and said, "Good job, Ben!" They laughed and played together after the work was done. They were good friends, and they knew that helping each other made them both feel great.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tom. Tom was a happy boy who loved to play. One day at school, Tom saw a tall girl named Lily. She was sad because she did not have a friend to play with. Tom wanted to help her. 

Tom ran over to Lily and said, "Do you want to play?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes!" They both began to skip around the playground. They laughed and had fun together. After playing, they sat down to do a drawing. Tom drew a big sun, and Lily drew a tall tree.

As they drew, more kids came to see. They liked Tom's sun and Lily's tree. Soon, all the kids wanted to draw together. They made a big picture with the sun and the tall tree. Everyone was happy! 

Tom learned that when you share and make friends, everyone can have fun. So, Tom and Lily skipped home, excited to play again tomorrow. They knew that making friends is the best way to be happy.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a girl named Emma. Emma was happy and liked to play with her friends. One day, her friend Mia looked sad. Emma walked over and said, “Mia, why are you sad?”

“I lost my toy,” Mia said with a frown. “It was my favorite toy!” Emma thought for a moment and said, “Let’s look for it together!” They walked through the warm school and looked in every room.

As they searched, they heard a sweet song from the music room. “Do you hear that song?” Emma asked. “Yes!” said Mia, smiling a little. “Let’s go see!” They ran to the music room and found their friend Sam singing with his guitar. 

“Hi, Sam!” said Emma. “We need help!” Sam stopped and asked, “What happened?” Emma told him about Mia’s lost toy. Sam said, “Let’s all look together!” So, they looked high and low. Finally, under a chair, they found the toy!

Mia was so happy! “Thank you, Emma! Thank you, Sam!” she cheered. Emma smiled and said, “We helped each other!” They all joined hands, danced, and sang the warm song together. They felt joy and friendship in their hearts.

Jack was at school with his friend, Mia. They were sitting on the floor, playing with a big puzzle. The puzzle had many bright colors and funny shapes. Jack said, “Look, Mia! I see a silly cat!” Mia laughed and said, “Yes! The cat is wearing a hat!”

Jack and Mia worked hard to fit the pieces together. Sometimes they made mistakes and put pieces in the wrong place. When that happened, they both laughed. Mia said, “Oh no! That piece does not fit there!” Jack giggled and said, “Let’s try again!”

After a little while, they finished the puzzle. It was a big sunny day with a big silly cat and its funny friends. Jack clapped his hands and said, “We did it, Mia! We made the puzzle!” Mia smiled and said, “Yes, we are a great team!”

Jack and Mia felt happy. They hugged each other and shared a big smile. “Let’s play again tomorrow!” said Jack. “Yes!” Mia said, laughing. They were best friends, and they loved to play together at school.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a girl named Sofia. Sofia was a happy girl who loved to paint. One day, her teacher said they could explore colors and make a big painting together. Sofia felt very proud to help her friends.

Sofia saw her friend, Max, looking sad. Max wanted to paint, but he did not know how to mix the colors. Sofia smiled and said, “Let’s explore together!” She took Max’s hand, and they found red, blue, and yellow paint. They mixed the colors and made green and purple. Max was happy!

Now, they painted a big tree with bright flowers. All their friends came to help. Sofia felt even prouder as they worked together. They laughed and shared the brushes. When they finished, the painting looked so nice!

At the end of the day, the teacher said, “What a beautiful painting!” Sofia and Max grinned from ear to ear. They learned that helping friends and exploring together makes them all happy. Sofia and Max felt proud of their work and each other. They left school with big smiles, excited for the next adventure!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Will. Will went to school every day. He loved to play with his friends. One sunny day, Will was looking for a new game to play. He was very excited!

Will walked around the big playground. He saw swings and slides, but he wanted to find something more fun. Then, he saw a big box in the grass. Will ran to the box and opened it. Inside, he found colorful balls and jump ropes! "Wow!" he said. "This is a great game!"

Will hurried to his friends. "Look what I found!" he shouted. His friends came over and smiled. They were very excited too! They all played with the balls and jump ropes together. They laughed and had so much fun.

At the end of the day, Will felt happy. He had discovered a new game and made his friends smile. Will loved school even more now!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Nora. Nora was at school, ready for her lesson. She was happy to learn, but then she heard a loud noise. It came from the big closet in the corner. Nora felt scared. “What is that noise?” she thought. 

Nora's teacher, Ms. Lee, saw her face. “Don’t worry, Nora. The closet is just a bit old,” Ms. Lee said with a smile. “Let’s finish our lesson, and then we can look together.” Nora took a deep breath and tried to be brave. 

After the lesson, Nora and Ms. Lee walked to the closet. They opened the door slowly. Inside, they found a little cat! The cat was scared, too. It had gotten lost and hidden in the closet. Nora felt happy she found the cat. 

Nora and Ms. Lee helped the cat. They gave it some food and water. Nora hugged the cat and said, “You are safe now!” The cat purred and looked happy. Nora was no longer scared. She learned that sometimes, things that seem scary can be wonderful surprises. And that made her smile!

Dan was a little boy. He loved to play with his friends at school. One day, Dan saw a girl sitting alone. She looked sad and lonely. Dan wanted to help her.

Dan walked over to the girl. "Hi," he said. "What is your name?" The girl looked up and said, "My name is Sue." Dan smiled and said, "Do you want to play with me?" Sue nodded and her face lit up with a smile.

They played together all day. Dan told a funny story and Sue laughed. They ran and jumped and had so much fun! Dan felt happy because he helped Sue. He did not want her to feel lonely anymore.

At the end of the day, Dan and Sue waved goodbye. Dan remembered how lonely Sue was, but now she was happy. They promised to play again soon. Dan felt warm inside because helping others made him feel good.

Ivy was a small girl. She went to school and liked to play with her friends. One day, Ivy felt very nervous. She had a big paper to show in front of the class. She did not want to talk. 

Ivy's friend, Mia, saw that Ivy was nervous. Mia said, “It is okay, Ivy! I will help you.” Mia smiled and took Ivy's hand. They stood together at the front of the class. 

Ivy held the paper and took a deep breath. She looked at Mia, and Mia gave her a thumbs up. Ivy began to talk about her paper. She felt happy because Mia was there with her. 

When Ivy finished, her friends clapped their hands. Ivy smiled wide. “Thank you, Mia!” she said. Ivy was not nervous anymore. She and Mia were great friends, and they helped each other at school.

One bright day, Kai went to school. He was very happy. "I can't wait to learn," he said with a big smile. He loved school!

In the classroom, Kai saw his friend, Mia. "Hi, Mia!" he said. "Are you ready to learn?" Mia nodded and said, "Yes! I want to learn about colors!"

The teacher, Miss Lily, came in. "Hello, class! Today we will learn about colors!" she said. Everyone cheered, "Yay!" Kai was so excited. He loved red, blue, and yellow.

After they learned about colors, Miss Lily gave them crayons. "Now you can draw!" she said. Kai drew a big red sun. "Look at my sun!" he said to Mia. "It makes me happy!"

At the end of the day, Kai ran to his mom. "I had a fun day at school!" he said. "I learned a lot and I am very happy!" His mom smiled and gave him a big hug. It was a lovely day!

Ruby was a little girl. She loved to go to school. Her classroom was bright and full of colors. There were blue walls and yellow chairs. Ruby liked to sit by the big window where the sun shined in.

One day, Ruby had a fun idea. She wanted to draw a big, happy sun for her classroom. She took her crayons and started to draw. She used bright yellow and big, round shapes. But when she looked at her sun, it was not perfect. Ruby felt sad and thought about stopping.

Then, her teacher came over. "Ruby," she said, "your sun is special. It is full of love and joy!" Ruby smiled. She learned that it is okay if things are not perfect. What matters is how we feel when we make them.

With this thought, Ruby finished her drawing. She showed it to her friends, and they all clapped. They added their own drawings too! The classroom became bright with many happy pictures. Ruby was so glad. She learned that sharing and trying is what makes us happy.

Finn was a little boy who loved to go to school. One sunny day, his teacher said, “Let’s read a fun book!” Finn smiled big. He loved to read. He sat on the rug and listened to the story. The book was about a kind dog who helped others.

After story time, it was time to go to the playground. Finn ran outside with his friends. “Let’s play tag!” he shouted. His friend Mia said, “Okay! I will be it!” Finn laughed and ran fast. They all had fun chasing each other.

While playing, Finn saw a girl sitting alone. She looked sad. Finn ran over and said, “Hi! Do you want to play with us?” The girl smiled and said, “Yes, I would like that!” So, they all played together and had the best time.

At the end of the day, Finn felt happy. He learned that being kind made everyone smile. Finn said to his friends, “Let’s always play together!” They all agreed and promised to share happiness on the playground.

Luna was a happy girl. She went to school every day. Her teacher, Miss Lily, was kind and nice. One day, it was very quiet in the classroom. The kids were busy doing their work.

Luna had a big idea. She wanted to write a story! She took her pencil and some paper. She thought about fun things. She thought about flowers and stars. Luna started to write. She wrote about a little cat who loved to play.

When she finished, she felt proud. Miss Lily came to see her. "What did you make?" she asked with a smile. Luna showed her the story. Miss Lily clapped her hands and said, "This is great, Luna! You are a good writer!" 

Luna felt happy in her heart. She loved to write. The kids in the class all cheered for her. They were all friends. Luna knew that school was fun and that writing could make everyone smile!

Owen was a shy boy. He liked to sit and draw with his pencil. But he did not like to play with other kids. Owen felt alone at school.

One day, while Owen sat under a big tree, he saw a girl run by. She was laughing and playing. Owen watched her for a while, but he did not say anything. Then, the girl tripped and fell. Owen jumped up and ran to her.

“Are you okay?” he asked. The girl smiled and said, “I am okay! Thank you!” Her name was Lily. Owen felt happy. They started to talk and share their crayons and pencils. They both liked to draw.

From that day on, Owen and Lily were friends. They played together every day. Owen was still shy, but he had fun with Lily. Now, he did not feel alone anymore. School was a happy place!

Maya was a happy girl. She loved to play with her friends at school. One day, Maya saw a big, colorful book on the shelf. It was new, and Maya’s eyes grew wide. She was very curious about what was inside.

Maya called her friend Sam. "Look at this book!" she said with a smile. Sam came over and looked too. "Wow! Let’s see what we can find!" he said. They opened the book and saw bright pictures of animals and fun games. Maya and Sam felt excited!

They played fun games from the book. They laughed and smiled as they pretended to be animals. Other friends saw them and wanted to join. Soon, all the kids were playing together. They shared the book and had so much fun.

At the end of the day, Maya felt happy. She had a special time with her friends. Maya loved to play, and she was thankful for the curious book that brought them all together. Everyone left school with big smiles.

Once upon a time, in a bright classroom, there was a boy named Cole. He was a brave boy who liked to play with his friends. One day, Cole saw a big desk with a lot of toys on it. He wanted to play with the toys, but there were many kids waiting to play too.

Cole thought for a moment. He wanted to take all the toys for himself, but he knew that was not nice. So, he decided to share the toys with his friends. “Let’s play together!” he said with a big smile. His friends were happy. They all came to the desk and played with the toys.

They took turns and had so much fun. They built towers with blocks and raced cars. Everyone laughed and played together. Cole felt good inside because he shared and made his friends smile. 

At the end of the day, all the kids thanked Cole for being brave and kind. Cole learned that sharing made playtime even better. He went home with a big smile, happy to have so many friends.

Chloe was a small girl with big, bright eyes. One sunny day at school, it was time for lunch. Chloe saw her friend, Tim, who looked sad. He had a small lunch and did not have enough food. Chloe wanted to help him.

Chloe thought for a moment. She looked in her lunch box. She had a big apple and some yummy cheese. She smiled and said, “Tim, do you want to share my lunch?” Tim's eyes got big, and he smiled back. “Yes, please!” he said happily.

Chloe gave Tim half of her apple and some cheese. They sat together on the grass and ate. Tim was very happy because now he had enough food. “Thank you, Chloe! You are a good friend,” he said with a big smile. 

After lunch, Chloe and Tim played games with other friends. They laughed and ran around. Chloe felt warm inside because she helped Tim, and they both had fun. At the end of the day, Chloe knew that sharing made everyone happy.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Jude. Jude had a gentle smile and a big blue backpack. He loved to help his friends. One day, his friend Lily felt sad because she lost her favorite toy. Jude wanted to help her.

Jude said, "Don’t worry, Lily! Let's look for it!" They walked around the school. They looked under the slide and behind the swings. Jude used his gentle voice to sing a happy song. The song made Lily smile. “Let’s keep looking!” she said with hope.

Suddenly, Jude heard a soft sound near the big tree. “What is that?” he asked. They ran to the tree, and there was Lily’s toy! Jude and Lily jumped with joy. “Yay!” they cheered. Jude felt very happy because he could help his friend.

After they found the toy, they went back to class. Jude and Lily sang the happy song together. All their friends joined in, and the classroom was full of laughter. Jude felt proud. He knew that helping others made the best days. And they all had a wonderful time.

Once upon a time in a bright school, there was a girl named Leah. Leah was very clever. She loved to draw and color with her crayon. One day, Leah had a big idea. She wanted to build a tall tower with her friends. 

Leah asked her friends, "Will you help me build a tower?" Her friends all said, "Yes!" They found big blocks and small blocks. They worked together and laughed as they built. Leah used her crayon to draw a funny face on the top block. Everyone giggled when they saw it.

But soon, the tower began to wobble. Leah felt a little sad. "Oh no! It might fall!" she said. But then she remembered to be brave and clever. "Let’s work together and hold it steady!" she said. Her friends listened and held the blocks tight.

Finally, they built the tallest tower ever! It was big and colorful. Leah and her friends cheered. They learned that when they work together, they can build something great. They were so happy, and they all hugged each other. That day, Leah knew that sharing and helping made fun things happen!

Tim was a happy boy. He loved to go to school every day. In school, there was a big library full of books. The library was a cheerful place. It had bright lights and many colors. 

One day, Tim found a box of paints in the library. He opened it and saw red, blue, and yellow paint. "Wow!" said Tim. "I can make art!" He took a brush and started to paint on a big white paper. The colors were bright and pretty!

While Tim was painting, a girl named Mia came in. She saw Tim and smiled. "Can I help?" she asked. Tim said, "Yes, please!" They began to paint together. They made a big rainbow with happy colors. 

When they finished, they looked at their art and laughed. It was the best painting ever! Tim and Mia became good friends that day. They loved to paint and play together in the cheerful library. The day ended with smiles, and they were both very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Anna. Anna was a friendly girl. She liked to play with her friends at school. Every day, they laughed and had fun together. 

One day, Anna had some homework to do. It was time to count. She had to count her red blocks and her blue blocks. Anna was a little sad because she did not want to do homework alone. She wanted to play with her friends instead.

But then, Anna had an idea! She asked her friend Sam to help her. "Will you help me count?" Anna asked. Sam smiled and said, "Yes!" Together, they sat on the floor and started to count the blocks. They counted one, two, three, and more! 

Anna and Sam had fun counting together. They giggled and helped each other. When they finished the homework, they felt happy. "Let’s play now!" Anna said. They ran outside to play with their other friends. They all smiled and laughed together. It was a great day at school!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Sam. Sam loved to go to school. Every day, he would skip to his classroom with a big smile. Sam liked to play and learn with his friends. They would draw pictures of flowers, trees, and even tall buildings!

One day, the teacher asked the class to make a special drawing. She said they could draw anything they wanted. Sam wanted to draw a tall tower. He took his crayons and started to work. But his friend, Mia, was sad. She wanted to draw, but she could not think of what to make.

Sam saw Mia with a frown. He had an idea! He skipped over to her and said, “Let’s draw together!” Sam helped Mia think of a fun picture. They decided to draw a big garden full of tall flowers. They both laughed and had fun while they drew side by side.

At the end of the day, the teacher looked at their drawings. She smiled and said, “You both did a great job!” Sam and Mia were happy. They learned that sharing and helping a friend is a wonderful thing to do. Sam skipped home with a big smile, knowing he had made a new friend and helped her too.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to play at school with her friends. She had a warm smile that made everyone feel happy. One sunny day, Lucy and her friend Sam sat under a big tree. They wanted to listen to a song.

Sam took out his little guitar. It was bright and blue. Lucy clapped her hands and said, “Play, Sam! Play a song!” Sam smiled and began to strum. The warm sun shone down on them, and the music filled the air. Lucy listened closely. She felt joy in her heart.

As the song played, other children came to join them. They danced and sang along. Everyone was laughing and having fun! Lucy felt so happy to be with her friends. The warm day and the sweet song made the school feel like a big hug.

When the song was over, everyone cheered! Lucy and Sam smiled at each other. They were glad to share their music and their fun. Lucy knew that with friends, every day could be a happy day. And they all played together until it was time to go home.

Jake was at school with his friend Mia. They sat on the soft rug with a big puzzle. The puzzle had bright colors and funny shapes. Jake smiled and said, “Let’s build it!” Mia nodded and said, “Yes! This will be fun!”

As they worked, Jake put a piece in the wrong spot. It was a silly mistake! Mia giggled and said, “That piece is blue, not green!” Jake laughed and said, “Oh no! I am silly!” They both laughed together. 

After a while, they found all the pieces. Mia found the last piece and said, “Look, Jake! We did it!” Jake jumped up and down. “Yay! We made the puzzle!” They hugged each other. 

The bell rang, and it was time to go home. Jake said, “I had fun today!” Mia smiled and said, “Me too! Let’s make another puzzle tomorrow!” They waved goodbye, happy to be friends.

Mia was a happy girl. She liked to help her friends at school. One day, Mia had a big idea. She wanted to explore the art room. She thought it would be fun to look at all the colors and things to make art!

Mia walked into the art room. She saw big brushes, bright paints, and soft paper. She picked up a blue brush and started to paint. She painted a big sun and happy flowers. As she painted, her friend Ben came in. He looked sad because he did not know how to paint.

Mia saw Ben and wanted to help him. "Come here, Ben! It is easy!" she said. Mia showed him how to use the brushes. Together, they painted a big picture of a rainbow. Mia felt very proud of their work. They made a great painting!

At the end of the day, their teacher saw the painting. She smiled and said, "Wow! This is beautiful!" Mia and Ben were so happy. They helped each other and made art together. They knew that helping friends makes the best fun!

Ben was a little boy who went to school. One sunny day, Ben felt very excited. He wanted to play a game with his friends. But when he looked around, he saw that no one was playing. Everyone seemed sad.

Ben thought for a moment. Then, he had a great idea! He decided to take out his big box of colorful balls. He wanted to share them with his friends. When he showed the balls, his friends smiled and came over. "Let’s play!" they said.

Together, they made a fun game with the balls. They threw them, kicked them, and ran all around. They laughed and played until it was time to go home. Ben felt very happy because he had discovered that sharing made everyone joyful.

At the end of the day, Ben learned something important. When you share, you can make new friends and have fun together. Ben was excited for another day at school, ready to play more games!

Zoe was a small girl. She was in her class at school. One day, Zoe had a big lesson about animals. She was excited at first, but then she felt scared. She thought the lesson would be hard, and she did not want to try.

Zoe sat at her desk. She looked at her teacher. The teacher smiled and said, "Let’s have fun with this lesson!" Zoe took a deep breath. She looked at the pictures of the animals. They were so cute! Zoe started to answer the questions. Each answer made her feel less scared.

When the lesson was over, Zoe was happy. She did not think it was hard at all! She learned that trying new things is fun. Zoe raised her hand and said, "I want to finish more lessons like this!" The other kids clapped for her.

Zoe learned that being scared is okay. It can change to happy if you try. She smiled and felt proud. At the end of the day, Zoe left school with a big smile, ready for more fun adventures!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Leo. He went to school every day. Leo had a big heart, but sometimes he felt lonely. He wanted to play with friends, but he did not know how to ask. One day, Leo saw a girl sitting by herself. She looked sad and lonely too.

"Do you want to play?" Leo asked the girl. She smiled and said, "Yes!" Leo was happy. They played games and shared toys. Leo liked to help his new friend, and they laughed a lot together. As they played, Leo remembered a story his mom told him about sharing and helping others.

The more they played, the less lonely they both felt. They found out that being kind and helpful made them feel good inside. Leo liked this very much! From that day on, Leo made sure to help anyone who felt lonely.

In the end, Leo and his friend made many more friends at school. They all played together and had fun. Leo learned that when he helped others, he was never lonely again. And they all lived happily ever after.

Ella was a little girl who went to school. One day, she felt very nervous. It was art time, and everyone had to use paper to make a nice picture. Ella saw her friend, Ben, looking sad. He did not have any paper. 

Ella wanted to help Ben. She took a big piece of her own paper and said, “Here, Ben! You can use this!” Ben smiled big and said, “Thank you, Ella!” Ella felt happy to help her friend. 

While Ben drew a big sun, Ella watched. She saw that he used bright colors. Then, she had an idea! She wanted to make a picture too. She found another piece of paper and used all the colors she liked. 

When the teacher came to look, she smiled. “Wow! You both made great art!” Ella felt proud and no longer nervous. She learned that helping others made her feel good. They both showed their art to the class, and everyone clapped!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Max. Max loved to go to school every day. At school, Max had many friends. They played games, sang songs, and learned new things together. Max was always happy in school!

One sunny day, Max saw a new boy. His name was Tim. Tim looked shy and sad. Max wanted to help. He walked over to Tim and said, "Hi! Do you want to play with us?" Tim smiled a little and said, "Yes, please!" Max was very happy to have a new friend.

Max and Tim played with blocks, drew pictures, and laughed a lot. They learned together and helped each other. Max showed Tim how to build a tall tower. Tim showed Max how to draw a big sun. They were both so happy to share and learn.

At the end of the day, Max and Tim looked at their tower and their pictures. They cheered and said, "We did it together!" Max learned that being a good friend is very nice. When you share and help, everyone can be happy. Max smiled big, knowing he had a fun day with his new friend.

Lily was a little girl. She loved school. Her classroom was bright. It had big windows, and the sun shone in. Lily liked to draw pictures of happy things like flowers and stars.

One day, Lily saw her friend Sam. Sam looked sad. He was trying to draw, but he was not happy with his picture. Lily wanted to help him. She said, "I can help you, Sam!" 

Lily took her crayons and sat next to Sam. She showed him how to draw a big, red apple. Sam smiled. He tried to draw the apple too. Together, they made bright pictures. The classroom was filled with laughter and happy noises.

At the end of the day, Sam said, "Thank you, Lily!" Lily felt very good inside. Helping her friend made her heart feel bright like the sun. They left the classroom with big smiles and their happy pictures.

Tom was a kind boy. He loved to go to school. One day, Tom went to the playground with his friends. They were happy to play together. 

“Let’s swing!” said Tom. The swings were big and fun. But then, Tom saw a girl sitting alone. She looked sad. 

“Why are you sad?” Tom asked. The girl said, “I want to play, but I don’t know how.” Tom had an idea. “You can come play with us!” he said with a smile.

So, Tom and his friends helped the girl. They played on the swings and made funny shapes in the sand. They even read a big book together. The girl laughed and smiled. 

At the end of the day, Tom felt happy. He helped a friend, and they all had fun. “I like school!” said Tom. And everyone agreed!

Emma was a little girl. She went to school every day. Her teacher was nice. The teacher helped Emma learn. Emma liked to write. She wrote in her book when the class was quiet.

One day, Emma had an idea. She wanted to write a story about a big, happy dog. She sat at her desk and started to write. The room was quiet, and she could think well. The teacher smiled at Emma and said, “Good job, Emma!”

When Emma finished her story, she wanted to share it. The teacher said, “Let’s have story time!” All the kids sat in a circle. Emma felt happy and a little shy. She read her story about the big, happy dog. The kids clapped when she was done.

Emma learned that sharing her writing was fun. She felt proud. The teacher said, “Always share your ideas, Emma. You have a big heart!” Emma smiled. She knew that writing and sharing made her day bright. And so, Emma went home happy, ready to write more stories.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jack. Jack was a shy boy. He liked to play with his toys, but he did not like to talk to many kids. One day, at school, Jack saw a bright pencil on the ground. It was yellow and shiny. 

Jack picked up the pencil. "Wow!" he said. He wanted to show it to his friends, but he felt shy. Instead, he decided to run to the playground. Maybe he could find someone to share it with there. 

At the playground, Jack saw a girl named Mia. She was sad because she lost her favorite toy. Jack ran to her and said, "Look! I found this pencil!" Mia's face lit up. "Thank you, Jack!" she said with a big smile. 

Jack felt happy. Mia was happy too. They started to play together. Jack learned that sharing made him feel good. From that day on, Jack was not so shy anymore. He had a new friend and his shiny yellow pencil!

Sofia went to school one sunny day. She was a curious girl. She loved to learn new things and play with friends. Today, her teacher said they could look at books. Sofia was so happy!

Sofia picked a big book with bright pictures. She opened the book and saw a fun story. She wanted to play a game that went with the story. She said, "Let’s play like the animals in the book!" Her friends liked the idea and they all began to act like the animals.

As they played, Sofia found a picture of a big, funny bear. The bear looked silly! Sofia said, "I want to be the bear!" Her friends laughed and joined her. They all pretended to be bears, jumping and dancing around the room.

At the end of the day, Sofia and her friends were very happy. They had a lot of fun playing and reading. Sofia smiled big and thought, "I love school!" She couldn’t wait to come back tomorrow for more adventures.

Will was a brave boy. He went to school every day. At school, there was a big desk. The desk was bright and shiny. Will liked to draw at the desk with his crayons.

One day, Will saw his friend, Mia, sitting alone. She looked sad. Will wanted to help. He walked over to Mia and said, "Do you want to share my desk?" Mia's eyes lit up. "Yes, please!" she said with a big smile.

Will and Mia sat at the desk together. They drew pictures of happy animals. Will drew a dog, and Mia drew a cat. They shared their crayons and laughed. Will felt good inside because he helped Mia feel happy.

When the bell rang, Will and Mia hugged. They felt brave and happy. They found out that sharing was fun. Will smiled and knew that helping others made school a better place!

Nora went to school with a big smile. It was a sunny day, and she was happy to see her friends. "Hi, friends!" she said. They all waved back. Nora had a small lunch with a sandwich and an apple. 

At lunchtime, Nora saw her friend Max. He looked sad. "What is wrong, Max?" Nora asked. Max said, "I do not have lunch. I am very hungry." Nora wanted to help. "You can share my lunch!" she said with a big grin. 

Max smiled back. "Really? Thank you, Nora!" They sat together and shared the small lunch. They ate the sandwich and the apple. After they finished, Max said, "That was yummy! You are a good friend!" 

Nora felt happy inside. "I like helping!" she said. They played together after lunch, laughing and having fun. That was a good day at school. Nora and Max were happy friends.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Dan. Dan had a gentle smile and a big, blue backpack. Every day, he walked to school with his backpack full of fun things. Dan loved to learn and play with his friends.

One sunny day, Dan saw his friend Lily sitting by herself. She looked sad. Dan went over to her and asked, "What is wrong, Lily?" She said, "I lost my toy, and I feel bad." Dan wanted to help. He smiled and said, "Let’s look for it together!"

Dan and Lily searched all around the big school. They looked in the playground, by the swings, and even in the grass. After a little while, they found Lily's toy under a slide! Lily was so happy that she jumped up and down. "Thank you, Dan!" she said with a big smile. 

To celebrate, Dan and Lily sang a happy song. Their voices were sweet and gentle, just like the sunshine. All their friends came to sing with them, and soon the whole school was full of joy. Dan felt warm inside because he helped his friend. Everyone was happy that day!

Ivy was a clever girl. She loved to play with her friends at school. One sunny day, Ivy saw her friend Ben. "Look, Ben!" she said. "Let’s build a big house with crayons!"

Ben smiled and said, "Yes! I love to build! What color should we use?" Ivy thought for a moment. "Let’s use red and blue and green!" she laughed. They took out the crayons and began to draw. Ivy drew the walls, and Ben drew the door and the roof.

As they worked, their friends came over. "What are you building?" asked Mia. "We are building a house!" said Ivy with a big grin. "Come help us!" Mia grabbed a yellow crayon, and they all joined in. They laughed and talked as they built their lovely house.

When they finished, the house was bright and happy. "Look at our house!" said Ben. Ivy clapped her hands. "It is so good! We all built it together!" They all felt warm inside because they worked as friends. They played in their crayon house all day, and everyone was happy.

Once upon a time, in a big, cheerful school, there was a boy named Kai. Kai loved to play and learn with his friends. One sunny day, their teacher took them to the library. The library was full of books and bright pictures. Kai loved to read, but today was special!

In the library, Kai found a big piece of paper and some bright colors. “I want to paint!” he said with a big smile. He took the colors and started to make a picture. He painted a big sun, green grass, and happy flowers. His friends watched him and thought it was great!

While Kai painted, he saw a book on the shelf that had pictures of other kids painting. He got the book and showed it to his friends. “Look! We can all paint together!” he said. They all took paper and colors and started to paint. The library was full of laughter and joy!

At the end of the day, they had many happy pictures to show. The teacher smiled and said, “You all did a great job!” Kai learned that sharing and having fun with friends makes everything better. They all went home with big smiles, ready to paint again another day.

Ruby was at school. She had a big smile on her face. Today, her friend Max came to play. "Hi, Max!" said Ruby. "Do you want to count with me?"

"Yes, Ruby! I love to count!" said Max. They sat on the floor and started to count the blocks. "One, two, three," said Ruby. Max clapped his hands. "You are a friendly friend, Ruby!" he said. Ruby giggled and said, "And you are a friendly friend too, Max!"

After counting, it was time for homework. Ruby said, "We can do our homework together!" Max nodded happily. They worked side by side, helping each other. They drew big circles and bright suns. "This is fun!" said Max. Ruby smiled and said, "Yes, friends help each other."

When they finished, Ruby and Max jumped up. "We did it!" they cheered. The teacher came by and said, "Good job, kids!" Ruby and Max high-fived each other. They were so happy to be friends. They left school with big smiles, ready to tell their parents about their fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Finn. Finn loved to skip on the grass at school. He had a big smile and liked to help his friends. One day, Finn saw his friend, Mia, sitting on the ground. She looked sad.

"What's wrong?" Finn asked. Mia said she lost her drawing. It was a tall tree with big green leaves. Finn wanted to help. He said, "Let’s look for it together!" So, they skipped around the playground, checking under swings and behind slides.

After a little while, they found the drawing stuck in a bush! Mia's face lit up with joy. "Thank you, Finn!" she said, hugging him tight. Finn felt happy to help his friend. They went back to class, and Mia showed everyone her tall tree drawing.

Finn smiled as he saw Mia's happy face. Helping others made him feel good inside. At school, they played and skipped together, and the day ended with lots of fun and laughter.

Luna was a happy girl. She loved to play with her friends at school. One sunny day, Luna felt warm and happy. She wanted to sing a song.

Luna found her friend Max. “Let’s sing a song!” she said with a big smile. Max smiled back and nodded. They both sat under a big tree. The sun shined down, making everything feel warm and nice.

Luna started to sing a fun song. Max listened and smiled. He loved her voice! Soon, other friends came over. They all listened and clapped their hands. The place was full of joy.

At that moment, Luna felt special. She saw how much her friends liked her song. They all sang together, and their voices made a sweet sound. Luna knew they were true friends, and they made each other happy. And that day, everyone felt warm inside.

Once upon a time, in a bright and happy school, there was a boy named Owen. Owen loved to play with his friends. One day, the teacher brought a big puzzle to class. It had many pieces, and they were all different colors. Owen thought it looked very silly!

Owen and his friends sat around the puzzle. They tried to put the pieces together, but it was hard. Some pieces did not fit where they thought they would. They started to feel a little sad. But then, Owen stood up and said, “Let’s laugh! This is a silly puzzle!” His friends looked at him and began to giggle.

When they laughed, they felt better! They worked together, and soon they found the right pieces. They put the puzzle together, and it was a picture of a big, happy sun. Everyone clapped and cheered, feeling proud of their work. Owen smiled and said, “We did it together!”

The moral of the story is this: It’s okay to have fun and laugh when things are hard. If we work together and help each other, we can make even the silliest puzzles right! And at the end of the day, everyone at school was happy.

One sunny day at school, Maya had a big smile. She walked into the art room and saw a big white paper on a table. “Wow!” Maya said. “I want to explore this painting!” She was very proud of her big idea.

Maya took out her colors. She had red, blue, and yellow. “I will use all my colors!” she said. She painted big stars and happy faces. Her friends watched and said, “Look at Maya! She is making a nice painting!” Maya felt even prouder.

After a while, Maya showed her painting to her teacher. “What do you think?” Maya asked. The teacher smiled and said, “This is great, Maya! You worked hard, and your painting is full of joy!” Maya jumped with glee. Her friends clapped their hands. 

Maya learned that when you explore and try your best, you can make something special. “I am proud of my painting and my friends!” she said. Everyone laughed and had fun together. It was a happy day at school!

Cole was happy to be at school. He ran to his class and said, "Hi, friends!" His friends waved and smiled back. Today was a special day. The teacher said they would play a new game. Everyone was excited!

After they sat down, the teacher showed them a big box. "Look inside!" she said. Cole peeked in the box and gasped. "Wow!" he said. "What is this?" Inside, there were colorful cards and little toys. "It’s a fun game!" said his friend Mia. 

Cole and Mia quickly took the cards out. "Let’s play!" said Cole. They shared the cards with all their friends. Everyone was so excited to discover how to play. They laughed and cheered as they took turns. 

At the end of the game, Cole smiled big. "That was the best!" he said. His friends all agreed. They hugged and went outside to play more. It was a happy day at school for Cole and his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Chloe. Chloe went to school with her friends. She loved to learn and have fun. One day, they had a lesson about colors. Chloe was happy to see all the bright colors! 

But then, some of the kids felt scared. They were worried they would not finish the lesson. Chloe saw her friend Sam looking sad. She said, "Don't be scared, Sam! We can do this together!" Sam smiled a little. 

Chloe and Sam worked hard. They helped each other name the colors. Soon, they finished the lesson! All the kids clapped and cheered. They were all very proud. 

At the end of the day, Chloe and Sam held hands. They laughed and felt happy. "We are good friends!" Chloe said. And they both agreed, friends make everything better!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jude. Jude was a quiet boy, and sometimes he felt lonely at school. One day, he sat by himself on the swing. He thought, "I wish I had a friend to play with."

His teacher, Miss Mia, saw him. She smiled and said, "Jude, do you remember the story I told? It was about a little bird that made a friend!" Jude nodded. He liked that story. It made him happy. Miss Mia continued, "Just like the bird, you can make friends too!"

Jude thought hard. Then, he saw a girl sitting alone on a bench. He walked over and said, "Hi! Do you want to play with me?" The girl smiled and said, "Yes! I would like that!" They played together and laughed in the sun.

At the end of the day, Jude felt very happy. He remembered that story and how the bird made a friend. He wasn’t lonely anymore. He said, "Thank you, Miss Mia! I have a friend now!" Miss Mia smiled and said, "You did it, Jude! Always remember that friendship makes us happy."

Leah was a little girl. She was at school with her friends. Today, Leah had a big job. She had to show everyone how to make a paper boat. But Leah felt nervous. What if she made a mistake?

Leah took a deep breath and smiled. She stood in front of the class and held up her paper. “Look!” she said. “We will fold the paper. It will be a boat!” Her friends looked at her with big eyes. They were ready to listen.

Leah showed them how to fold the paper. She went slow so everyone could see. Some friends helped each other. They laughed and had fun. One friend said, “I need help!” Leah came over and showed him again. 

When they were done, all the kids had paper boats. They raced them in the water outside. Leah was so happy! She didn't feel nervous anymore. Everyone was smiling, and they all said, “Thank you, Leah!” It was a great day at school.

Tim was a little boy. He went to school every day. Today was a sunny day. Tim was very happy! 

At school, Tim saw his friend, Sue. "Hi, Sue!" said Tim. "Are you ready to learn?" Sue smiled and said, "Yes, Tim! I love to learn." 

The teacher, Mr. Lee, came in. "Good morning, class!" he said. "Today, we will learn about colors!" Tim jumped up. "Yay! I like colors!" 

Mr. Lee showed them red, blue, and green. "Can you say red?" asked Mr. Lee. "Red!" shouted the kids. They all laughed and clapped their hands. 

When school was over, Tim and Sue ran outside. "That was fun!" said Tim. "I am so happy!" Sue nodded. "Me too, Tim!" They smiled big smiles, ready for more fun days at school.

One sunny day, Anna went to her bright classroom. The walls were yellow, and the sun shone through the big windows. Anna loved her classroom! She said to her friend, “I want to draw a big flower!”

Her friend Max smiled and said, “That sounds fun! Let’s draw together!” Anna took out her big paper and bright crayons. She drew a tall flower with a big smile. Max drew a little bee flying around the flower.

When their teacher saw their drawings, she clapped her hands and said, “Wow! These are so bright and pretty! You both did a great job!” Anna and Max felt happy. They learned that sharing and working together makes everything better.

At the end of the day, Anna said, “I love drawing with you, Max!” Max replied, “I love drawing with you too, Anna!” They smiled at each other, feeling good inside. They knew that friends make fun things even more fun.

Sam was a kind boy. One sunny day, he went to school with his big backpack. He liked to learn and play with his friends. After his class, it was time to go to the playground. Sam was very excited!

At the playground, Sam saw a big book on the grass. He looked around but did not see anyone. “I wonder who lost this?” Sam said to himself. He picked up the book and opened it. The pages were full of fun pictures and nice words. Sam could not wait to read!

As he read, some of his friends came to see what he found. “What is it, Sam?” they asked. “It is a book!” Sam said with a smile. He showed them the pictures. All the kids sat down and listened as Sam read the story out loud. They laughed and had fun together.

When the story was done, Sam felt happy. He had shared the book with his friends, and they all liked it. They decided to go back to class and tell the teacher about their fun time. Sam was so glad he found the book at the playground. It made his day special, and he knew the playground was a magic place!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy was a happy girl. She loved to play with her friends at school. One day, her teacher said, "Let’s write a story!" Lucy felt excited. 

The teacher said to be quiet while we write. Lucy sat at her desk and took out her big paper and a bright crayon. She looked at her friend, Mia. They smiled at each other. They both loved to write stories together. 

Lucy wrote about a big, blue bird. Mia wrote about a funny cat. They shared their stories and giggled. The teacher said, "Good job, girls!" Lucy felt proud. 

At the end of the day, Lucy and Mia were happy. They had fun writing and being friends. They promised to write more stories together. It was a great day at school!

Jake was a shy boy. He liked to sit alone and draw with his pencil. He liked to draw big trees and happy suns. But one day, he saw a friend named Lily. She looked sad. Jake wanted to help, but he was too shy to talk.

Jake took a deep breath. He held his pencil tight and ran over to Lily. “What’s wrong?” he asked softly. Lily said, “I lost my favorite toy. I can't find it.” Jake thought for a moment. Then he had an idea!

“Let’s look together!” Jake said with a smile. They ran through the school, looking behind tables and in the hallway. Finally, they found the toy under a big chair! Lily’s face lit up with joy. “Thank you, Jake!” she said. 

Jake felt happy too. Helping Lily made him smile. From that day on, he learned that being shy was okay, but helping others was even better. They played together with the toy and felt like good friends.

Mia was a curious girl. She loved to play with her friends at school. One sunny day, Mia saw a big book on the shelf. The book was bright and had many pictures. Mia wanted to see what was inside.

Mia took the book down and opened it. The pages were full of fun stories and cute animals. Mia felt happy and said, “Wow! Look at this!” Her friend Leo came over to see. “Can I play too?” Leo asked with a smile. Mia nodded, and they began to read together.

As they turned the pages, Mia and Leo found a story about a brave cat and a silly dog. They laughed and made funny sounds. The two friends enjoyed the story very much and wanted to read more. They took turns reading out loud and pointing at the pictures.

At the end of the day, Mia and Leo put the book back on the shelf. They were now best friends. Mia said, “Let’s play together again tomorrow!” Leo agreed, and they both walked out of school with big smiles. They were happy to have found a fun book and each other.

One day at school, Ben sat at his desk. He was a brave little boy. Ben liked to help his friends. Today, he saw his friend Lucy looking sad. She did not have a crayon to draw.

Ben thought for a moment and then had a great idea. He smiled and said, “Lucy, I will share my crayons with you!” Lucy's face lit up with joy. She said, “Thank you, Ben! You are very kind!”

Together, they drew bright pictures. Ben drew a big sun, and Lucy drew a happy dog. As they drew, they talked and laughed. Ben felt happy to help his friend. 

When the bell rang, Ben and Lucy were still smiling. They showed their pictures to the class. Everyone liked their art! Ben learned that sharing is good and helps friends. He was brave to help, and now he and Lucy were happy together.

Zoe was a small girl with a big heart. One sunny day at school, it was time for lunch. Zoe saw her friend Mia sitting all alone. Mia looked sad and did not have any food. Zoe wanted to help her.

Zoe smiled and said, “You can share my lunch!” Zoe had a small sandwich and some fruit. Mia’s face lit up with joy. Together, they sat under a tree and ate their lunch. They shared laughs and stories as they enjoyed their food.

After lunch, Zoe and Mia played games with their other friends. They all had fun together! Zoe felt happy because she helped Mia feel better. 

The moral of the story is that sharing and helping friends can make everyone smile!

One day at school, Leo had a big, bright backpack. He loved his backpack. It was blue with big red stars! “Look at my backpack!” Leo said to his friend Mia. Mia smiled and said, “Wow, Leo! It is so cool!”

Later, the teacher said, “Let’s sing a song!” The children were excited. Leo liked to sing. He had a gentle voice, just like a soft breeze. “I can sing!” he said with a big smile. Mia clapped her hands and said, “Yes, let’s sing together!”

When they started to sing, Leo felt happy. The song was about the sun and the sky. “The gentle sun shines bright!” Leo sang. The other kids sang along. They sang loud and soft, and everyone was smiling!

After the song, the teacher said, “Great job, everyone!” Leo was so proud. He looked at Mia and said, “Did you like it?” She nodded and said, “Yes! You sing so nice, Leo!” Leo felt warm inside. He loved school, his backpack, and singing with his friends. It was a happy day!

One sunny day at school, Ella brought her favorite crayon. It was red and sharp. “I want to build a big house!” said Ella with a big smile. She looked at her friends and said, “Do you want to help me?” 

“Yes! I want to help!” said Max, a clever boy with curly hair. Lily, a girl with a bright dress, jumped up and said, “Me too!” The three friends took out blocks of all colors. They stacked the blocks high and made a tall house.

As they built, they laughed and shared. Ella said, “Let’s make the roof blue!” Max said, “And a garden with green!” Lily added, “And a door with pink!” They worked hard and made the best house. 

When they finished, Ella said, “Look at our house! It is so pretty!” Everyone clapped. Their teacher came over and smiled. “You are all clever builders! You worked together so well!” The friends felt happy. 

The moral of the story is that working together is fun, and you can make great things with friends!

Max went to school. He was happy because it was a sunny day. “I love school!” he said with a big smile. Today, Max's class would go to the library.

In the library, Max saw many books. They were big and small. His teacher said, “You can pick a book to read!” Max chose a cheerful book with bright colors. He loved the pictures inside. “Look at this!” he said to his friend, Mia. “It is so fun!”

After reading, Max's teacher said, “Now we will paint!” Max jumped up and said, “Yay! I love to paint!” They took out big sheets of paper and bright paints. Max painted a big sun. “I am painting a cheerful sun!” he told Mia. She painted flowers that were pink and yellow.

When they were done, Max looked at his painting. “It makes me smile!” he said. The teacher clapped her hands. “Very good, everyone! Your art is so cheerful!” Max felt proud. He had a great day at school, with books and paint. He went home happy.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She was very friendly and loved to play with her friends at school. One sunny day, Lily had some homework to do. She looked at her paper and said, “Oh no! I have to count the stars!”

Lily sat at her desk and began to count. “One, two, three...” she said softly. Her friend Max came over. “What are you doing, Lily?” he asked with a big smile. Lily looked up and said, “I am counting stars for my homework!”

Max wanted to help. “Can I count with you?” he asked. Lily said, “Yes! Let’s count together!” So they counted the stars. “Four, five, six...”

After they finished, Lily was happy. “We did it! Thank you for helping me, Max!” she said. Max smiled back, “You are a great friend, Lily!” They both felt good and went outside to play, laughing and playing all day long.

One sunny day at school, Tom had a big smile. He loved to play with his friends. “Let’s go outside!” he said. His friends shouted, “Yes! Let’s skip!” 

Tom and his friends all skipped out to the big yard. They skipped over grass and flowers. “Look at that tall tree!” said Tom. “It is so big!” His friend Lily pointed and said, “I want to make a drawing of it!”

After they played, they sat on the grass. Tom took out a bright blue crayon. “I will help you, Lily!” he said. They both started to draw the tall tree. “It looks great!” said Tom. Lily smiled big and said, “Yes, it does!”

When they finished, they ran to their teacher and showed her the drawing. “Wow! What a nice tree!” she said. Tom and Lily felt happy. They all skipped back inside, laughing and playing. It was a perfect day at school!

Once upon a time, in a warm school, there was a girl named Emma. Emma loved to play with her friends. Every day, after they played, they would sit together on the soft grass. They liked to listen to the birds sing their happy song. 

One day, Emma's friend, Mia, felt sad. She did not want to play. Emma saw this and said, “Let’s listen to the song of the birds!” Mia smiled a little. Emma took Mia’s hand, and they sat together. 

As they listened to the sweet song, the sun shone down, making everything warm. Emma sang a little with the birds. “You can sing too!” she said to Mia. Mia started to sing, and soon they both were singing together. 

After a while, Mia felt happy again. “Thank you, Emma!” she said. “You make me feel good!” Emma smiled back. They hugged and ran off to play with their other friends. The warm sun and the sweet song made their day bright and fun!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jack. Jack went to school and loved to play with his friends. One day, they found a big puzzle on the floor. It was a silly puzzle with colors and funny shapes.

Jack and his friends looked at the puzzle. They wanted to put it together, but they did not know how to start. Jack saw that one friend, Lily, looked sad. She really wanted to help, but she did not know what to do.

Jack smiled at Lily and said, "Let’s work as a team!" Lily smiled back. Jack and his friends held hands and started to put the pieces together. They laughed when they found a piece that did not fit. "Silly piece!" they said together, and they giggled.

At last, they finished the puzzle! It was bright and happy. Jack, Lily, and their friends jumped up and down. They helped each other, and now they had a big, silly puzzle to show! Everyone was happy, and they all laughed together. It was a great day at school!

Sofia woke up with a big smile. Today was a special day at school. She was going to paint with her friends. Sofia loved to paint! She felt proud when she saw her art.

At school, Sofia saw her friends, Mia and Tom. They were happy too! "Let’s explore!" said Sofia. They ran to the art room and saw all the bright colors. Red, blue, and yellow were everywhere. 

Sofia picked up a brush and dipped it in blue. She made big waves on her paper. Mia painted a big sun, and Tom drew a green tree. They laughed and helped each other. Sofia felt proud of their paintings. They were like a team! 

When they finished, their art looked so nice together. The teacher smiled and said, "Great job, kids!" Sofia, Mia, and Tom felt so happy. They were proud of their art, and they knew they were great friends.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Will. Will was a happy boy who loved to play. One day at school, he found a big box. Will was very excited! He opened the box and saw many fun toys inside. There were balls, blocks, and a cool game!

Will wanted to play first, but he thought of his friends. "I can share!" he said with a big smile. Will called his friends, and they all came to see. They were so happy to discover the toys. Everyone wanted to play together. Will was glad he shared his fun!

As they played the game, they laughed and cheered. They took turns and helped each other. If someone fell, the others helped them up. Will felt warm in his heart because sharing made everyone smile. It was a great time!

At the end of the day, Will and his friends felt good. They learned that sharing is a nice thing to do. Will said, "I love playing with you all!" They all agreed, and they left school happy, knowing that helping others makes the fun even better!

Nora was a little girl who went to school. One day, she had a big lesson about shapes. Nora felt a little scared because she thought the lesson would be hard. She did not want to make a mistake. 

The teacher told the class, “Don’t worry! We will have fun!” Nora looked at her friends, and they all smiled. They started to learn about circles, squares, and triangles. As they practiced, Nora felt better. She began to see that the shapes were not so hard after all!

When the lesson was almost over, Nora worked very hard. She drew a big circle and a green square. She felt proud when she finished her work. “I did it!” she said with joy. Her teacher clapped and said, “Great job, Nora!”

Nora learned that trying new things can be fun. It is okay to feel scared at first. When she finished her lesson, she felt happy and proud. Nora went home with a big smile, ready for her next big adventure!

Once upon a time, in a big school, there was a boy named Dan. Dan liked to play, but sometimes he felt very lonely. He would sit by himself and watch other kids play. One day, a girl named Mia came to him. “Hi, Dan! Why are you sitting here all alone?” she asked with a smile.

Dan looked at her and said, “I don’t know how to play with the others. I feel lonely.” Mia thought for a moment and said, “Do you want to hear a story? I can tell you one about friendship!” Dan’s eyes got big. “Yes, please!” he said, happy to hear a story.

Mia began, “Once there was a little bird. The bird felt lonely too. One day, the bird saw other birds playing. It was shy, but it wanted to join. The bird took a deep breath and said, ‘Can I play with you?’ The other birds smiled and said, ‘Yes!’ The lonely bird became happy and had fun!” 

When Mia finished, Dan smiled big. “I can remember that!” he said. “I can ask to play too!” Then, Dan took a deep breath and said to the kids, “Can I play with you?” The kids smiled and said, “Yes!” Dan felt happy now. He learned that it is good to remember to ask and that he is not alone anymore.

Ivy was a small girl. She went to school every day. Today, Ivy felt a bit nervous. She had a big paper to show to her class. The paper had nice drawings and bright colors.

When it was her turn, Ivy walked to the front of the room. Her hands shook a little. She took a deep breath and smiled. “I will show you my paper!” she said. The other kids looked at her with big eyes.

Ivy held up the paper. “Look at my sun! And my tree!” she pointed. The kids cheered and clapped. Ivy's nervousness went away, and she felt happy. 

After she finished, her teacher smiled and said, “Very good, Ivy!” Ivy beamed with joy. She was proud of her work, and she knew she could do it again. School was fun, and she loved to show her art!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Kai. He went to school every day. At school, Kai was happy. He loved to learn new things. He learned about colors, shapes, and numbers. 

One day, Kai saw his friend Mia. Mia looked sad. She did not know how to tie her shoes. Kai wanted to help. He said, “Don’t worry, Mia! I can help you learn.” Mia smiled a little bit and nodded.

Kai showed Mia how to make a loop and pull it tight. It was fun! Mia tried, and soon she learned to tie her shoes all by herself. “Yay!” she cheered. Kai was happy to help his friend. 

At the end of the day, all the kids played together outside. They laughed and had fun. Kai felt so good inside because he could help Mia. School was a great place to learn and make friends. Everyone was happy!

Ruby was in her bright classroom. The sun shone through the window. Ruby loved to draw. One day, her friend Sam sat next to her. Sam looked sad.

“Why are you sad, Sam?” Ruby asked. Sam said, “I can’t draw a car.” Ruby smiled and said, “Let me help you!” She took a big piece of paper and began to draw a car. 

“This is how you do it!” Ruby said. “First, you draw a big box.” Sam watched closely. Ruby drew a big box and then four wheels. “Now you try!” she said. Sam took a crayon and drew his box. 

Sam looked happy now! “Thank you, Ruby!” he said. They both drew bright cars together. The classroom was full of smiles. Ruby felt good helping her friend. They had so much fun!

Finn was a kind boy who loved to play. One sunny day at school, Finn went to the playground. He ran to the swings and smiled. He liked to swing high in the sky. 

After playing on the swings, Finn saw a big book in the grass. He picked it up and looked at the cover. The book had bright pictures and big letters. Finn was excited! He wanted to read it.

He sat down on the grass and opened the book. The story was about a brave dog and a happy cat. Finn read the words slowly and smiled. He loved the tale of friendship. 

When Finn finished the book, he saw his friends coming. “Look! I found a book!” he said with joy. Together, they sat and listened as Finn read the story to them. They all laughed and clapped. It was a happy day at school!

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a girl named Luna. She loved to learn new things. One day, her teacher said, “Let’s all write a story!” The room was quiet as all the kids got ready. Luna smiled and opened her little notebook.

Luna looked at her friends. “What will you write?” she asked. Her friend Tim said, “I will write about a big dog.” Another friend, Mia, said, “I want to write about a happy cat!” Luna thought for a moment and said, “I will write about the moon and stars!” 

As they wrote, the teacher walked around. She smiled and said, “You are all doing so well! Keep going!” Luna wrote and wrote. She felt so happy inside. When they were done, the teacher said, “Now, let’s share our stories!” 

All the kids read their stories out loud. Luna felt proud. Everyone clapped and cheered. The teacher smiled and said, “You are all great writers!” Luna learned that sharing is fun and that being quiet helps us think. 

At the end of the day, Luna went home with a big smile on her face. She loved her school and her friends. And she couldn’t wait to write more stories!

Owen was a shy boy. He liked to draw but did not like to talk to others. One day at school, Owen took out his pencil and started to draw a big, bright sun. The sun had a smile and looked very happy. 

While Owen was drawing, some kids saw his nice picture. They said, “Wow! That is a great sun!” Owen felt happy but still shy. He wanted to play with them, but he was scared. Then, a little girl named Mia said, “Come and show us your art!” 

Owen took a deep breath and decided to run to the group. He showed them his sun, and they all smiled. “Let’s draw together!” Mia said. Owen joined in, and soon they were all drawing fun pictures. Owen felt good and happy to be with friends. 

The moral of the story is that sometimes it is good to be brave and share. When Owen ran to show his art, he made new friends and had a great time.

Maya was a curious girl. She loved to play at school with her friends. One sunny day, her teacher brought a big, colorful book to the class. Maya's eyes sparkled as she looked at the book. She wanted to see what was inside!

After the class finished, Maya asked her friends, “Can we play with the book?” They all said yes and sat in a circle. As they opened the book, they saw pictures of big animals and tall trees. Maya pointed and said, “Look! A lion!” Her friends gasped with joy.

While they played, Maya saw a picture of a little girl in the book. The girl was reading and smiling. Maya felt happy and thought, “I want to read too!” She ran to the teacher and asked, “Can I learn to read?” The teacher smiled and said, “Yes, Maya! You can learn!”

From that day, Maya played and learned. She read many books and found new stories. She was not just curious; she was also smart! Maya showed her friends how fun reading was, and they all became happy readers too. In the end, they learned that with curiosity and play, they could discover the joy of reading together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Cole. Cole was a brave boy. He liked to play with his friends at school. Cole sat at his special desk. It was blue and had stickers on it. 

One day, Cole saw his friend Mia. She looked sad. Cole wanted to help her. He knew that friends should share. So, he said, “Mia, do you want to play with my toys?” Mia smiled and nodded. 

Cole and Mia played together at his desk. They shared the toys and had so much fun. They laughed and made silly sounds. All the other kids joined them, too. 

At the end of the day, Cole felt happy. He was brave to share, and now he had more friends. Cole learned that sharing makes everyone smile!

Chloe was a small girl. She liked to help her friends at school. One sunny day, it was time for lunch. Chloe took out her small lunchbox. It was bright red and had a happy face on it.

“Wow, Chloe! Your lunchbox is so nice!” said Max, her friend. “What do you have in there?”  
“I have a sandwich and an apple!” Chloe said with a big smile. “Do you want to share?”

Max looked sad. “I forgot my lunch at home,” he said. “I am very hungry!”  
Chloe thought for a moment. “I can help! You can have some of my sandwich!” she said. 

Max’s face lit up. “Thank you, Chloe! You are very kind!” He took a bite of the sandwich. “Yummy!” he said. They both laughed and enjoyed their meal together. 

In the end, Chloe felt happy. She learned that helping friends makes everyone smile. “Let’s always help each other,” said Chloe. Max nodded. “Yes! Helping is nice!” They finished their lunch and played happily.

Once upon a time, in a sunny school, there was a boy named Jude. Jude had a bright blue backpack. It was big and nice, and he loved to carry it. One day, while Jude played on the swings, he saw his friend Mia. Mia looked sad. 

“Why are you sad, Mia?” Jude asked. Mia said, “I lost my toy. I can’t find it!” Jude wanted to help. “Let’s look together!” he said with a big smile. They both looked around the swings, under the slide, and near the big tree. They looked and looked, but the toy was not there. 

Then Jude had an idea. “Let’s sing a happy song!” he said. Jude began to sing a gentle song about toys and friendship. Mia listened and started to smile. Soon, other kids heard Jude and joined in. They sang together, and it made everyone feel good. 

Just then, a little girl named Lily came running. “I found your toy!” she said, holding it up high. Mia’s face lit up with joy. “Thank you, Lily!” she cheered. Jude and Mia hugged. They were so happy. Jude said, “We helped each other today!” And they all sang their gentle song together, feeling warm and happy in the school.

Leah was a clever girl. She loved school and loved to play with her friends. One sunny day, Leah wanted to build a big tower with her crayons. She had red, blue, and green crayons. They were bright and fun!

Leah saw her friend Tim. Tim looked sad. “What is wrong?” Leah asked. Tim said he could not find his crayons. Leah wanted to help. She said, “Let’s build together!” Tim smiled. Leah gave him some crayons, and they started to build a tall tower.

The tower grew higher and higher. Leah and Tim worked together, stacking the crayons. Other friends saw and came to help too. They all laughed and cheered as the tower got taller and taller. Soon, the tower was so big that everyone was happy!

At the end of the day, Leah, Tim, and their friends stood by the big tower. They felt proud. They learned that helping each other makes playtime more fun. And Leah was so glad she could help her friend. They all had a happy day at school!

Tim was a happy little boy. One day, he went to school. The sun shone bright, and the sky was blue. Tim was cheerful. He loved to play with his friends and learn new things.

In school, Tim's teacher said, "Today, we will go to the library!" Tim clapped his hands. He liked books. They were full of fun pictures and stories. When they got to the library, Tim saw lots of colors. "Look at the paint!" he said with a big smile. The walls were bright and cheerful!
