Tim's teacher said, "Let’s paint a picture.” Tim and his friends took brushes and paint. They painted flowers, trees, and the sun. “I love painting!” Tim said. “Me too!” said his friend, Mia. They laughed and made a big mess, but it was so much fun!

When they finished, Tim looked at their cheerful pictures. “Wow! We did a great job!” he said. Everyone cheered. Tim felt happy inside. The teacher said, “You all are great artists!” Tim smiled and felt proud. The day was perfect, and Tim loved school.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Anna. She was a friendly girl who loved to play with her friends at school. One day, the teacher gave everyone some homework. The homework was to count all the apples in the kitchen. Anna was excited!

After school, Anna went home with her friend Max. They wanted to finish their homework together. They went to the kitchen and saw many red, green, and yellow apples! “Let’s count them!” Anna said. They counted and counted. “One, two, three… all the way up to ten!” shouted Max. They were happy to see so many apples.

When they finished counting, Anna had an idea. “Let’s share some apples with our other friends!” she said with a big smile. Max thought this was a great idea. They took the apples and went to find their friends.

All the friends were so happy to get an apple. They laughed and played together. Anna felt great because sharing made everyone smile. The moral of the story is that sharing makes us all happy. And then, they went home feeling good.

Sam was a little boy. He was very happy at school. One sunny day, Sam liked to skip on the playground. He skipped here and there, feeling the soft grass under his feet. 

As he skipped, Sam saw a tall girl named Mia. Mia was sad. She had a big drawing in her hands, but it was torn. Sam wanted to help her. He ran over and smiled. “What happened?” he asked. 

Mia said, “I wanted to show my drawing to the class, but it got ripped.” Sam thought for a moment. He said, “We can fix it together!” They found some tape and gently stuck the pieces back together. 

When they finished, Mia smiled big. “Thank you, Sam!” she said. Now her drawing was nice again. They both skipped to class, happy to share. The teacher said, “Great job, Sam and Mia!” They were proud and had fun that day.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lucy. Lucy went to school every day. One sunny day, the sun was warm, and the sky was blue. Lucy walked into her classroom with a big smile.

At school, Lucy heard a sweet song. It was coming from the music room. She wanted to listen, so she tiptoed to the door. The song was happy, and it made her feel good inside. Lucy wondered what made the song so nice.

When she peeped inside, Lucy saw her teacher and friends singing with big smiles. They were all clapping and dancing together. Lucy's heart was full of joy. She wanted to join them! 

Lucy opened the door wide and said, "Can I sing too?" Her friends cheered, "Yes, Lucy!" So, she joined in. They all sang the song together, and the warm sun shone through the windows. 

Lucy felt very happy to be with her friends. They sang and danced until the bell rang. That day, Lucy discovered how fun it is to sing and be with friends. She went home with a big smile, ready to tell her family about the warm song and her happy day at school.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jake. Jake loved to play at school. One day, his teacher gave the class a big puzzle to do. It had many pieces, and some pieces were very silly! Jake looked at the pieces and giggled. He thought it was funny how they did not fit together.

Jake tried to put the pieces in the right place with his friends. They worked hard, but it was still tricky. Sometimes, they made silly mistakes. When a piece did not fit, they all laughed together. Their laughter made them feel happy. Jake loved being with his friends.

After a lot of giggles and fun, they finally finished the puzzle! It looked bright and colorful. Jake and his friends jumped up and down. They felt proud because they did it together. 

The lesson was clear: it is okay to be silly and make mistakes. Working with friends and having fun is what matters the most. Jake smiled big, knowing that laughter makes everything better. And they all lived happily, ready for their next puzzle!

One sunny day at school, Mia was excited. She held her paintbrush and said, "I want to explore today!" Her teacher smiled and said, "That’s a great idea, Mia! Let’s make a big painting together!"

Mia dipped her brush in red paint. "Look! I can make a big red sun!" she said with a big smile. Her friends joined in. "I will make green grass," said Sam. "I want to paint a blue sky!" said Lily. They all worked hard. 

After some time, the big painting was done. Mia looked at it and felt proud. "We made a great painting!" she said. Her friends cheered, "Yay! We did it!" The teacher clapped her hands and said, "This is the best painting ever!"

Mia and her friends were happy. They had explored, painted, and worked together. It was a fun day at school, and when they showed the painting to their families, everyone smiled and said, "Good job!" Mia felt very proud.

Ben was a little boy. He loved to go to school. One sunny day, Ben felt very excited. Today was a special day! He was going to play with his friends.

At school, Ben found a big box. He opened it and was surprised! Inside, he saw a new game. It had bright colors and fun pieces. “Wow!” Ben said. “I discover a game!” He ran to tell his friends.

Ben’s friends came to see. They all smiled and jumped. "Can we play?" they asked. Ben nodded and they all sat down together. They took turns and laughed a lot. They played the game and had so much fun!

At the end of the day, Ben felt happy. He loved the game, but he loved his friends more. They all played together and had a great time. Ben was glad he discovered the game, but he was even more glad to share it with his friends. They all went home smiling.

Zoe was at school. It was time for a lesson. Zoe sat at her desk and listened to her teacher. The teacher smiled and said, “Today, we will learn how to help each other!” Zoe liked this idea. 

But then Zoe saw her friend Tim. Tim looked scared. He did not understand the lesson. Zoe wanted to help him. She waved her hand and said, “Tim! I can help you!” Tim smiled a little, but he still looked worried. 

Zoe moved to Tim’s desk. She showed him the simple pictures and explained what they meant. Tim began to understand. They worked together, and soon, Tim was happy. “Thank you, Zoe!” he said with a big smile. 

When the lesson was over, Zoe felt proud. She had helped a friend. Tim said, “Let’s be friends forever!” Zoe smiled back. They finished the lesson together and had a great day. School was fun!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Leo. Leo went to school every day. He liked his school, but sometimes he felt lonely. He wanted to play with friends. One day, Leo saw his friends playing a fun game. He wanted to join, but he felt shy.

Leo sat on the bench and watched. Then, he remembered a story his mom told him. It was about sharing and being brave. Leo thought, “I can be brave too!” So, he got up and walked over to his friends. He said, “Can I play too?”

The friends smiled and said, “Yes!” They let Leo join the game. Leo felt happy and not lonely anymore. They ran and laughed together. Leo learned that it’s good to be brave and to share fun times. 

At the end of the day, Leo went home with a big smile. He remembered how good it felt to play with friends. From that day on, he was never shy again. Leo always knew that being brave made him happy.

Ella was a little girl who went to school. She liked school very much. One day, her teacher, Miss Sue, asked the class to show their art on paper. Ella wanted to show her drawing, but she felt nervous. "What if they don’t like my picture?" she thought.

When it was Ella’s turn, she took a deep breath. “I can do this!” she said to herself. She held up her paper and said, “This is my rainbow!” The kids in the class looked at her drawing. They smiled and clapped their hands. “Wow, Ella! It’s so pretty!” said her friend Ben. Ella felt happy and the nervous feelings went away.

After everyone showed their work, Miss Sue said, “Ella, you made a great rainbow! Always share your work, even when you feel nervous.” Ella nodded and felt proud. She learned that showing her art was fun, and it was okay to feel nervous sometimes.

From that day on, Ella showed her art with a big smile. She knew that sharing made her happy. And she was not nervous anymore. The end!
